card.intuition: A metric that measures the percentage of your source code executed during testing. It helps identify 'dark corners' of the app that have no tests at all.
card.analogy: A coloring book: the source code is the drawing. Every time a test runs over a line, it colors it in. If you finish your tests and half the page is still white, your tests haven't 'visited' those parts of the code yet.
when_to_use: Identifying risky areas of the code that are completely untested
Gamifying the testing process to encourage developers to write more tests
Setting a 'CI Gate' (e.g., 'Build fails if coverage is below 80%')
gotchas: Coverage != Quality: you can have 100% coverage with tests that assert NOTHING. Coverage tells you which lines were 'Touched', not which lines were 'Verified'.
Diminishing Returns: going from 0% to 80% is great; going from 95% to 100% is often very hard and not worth the effort.

exercise.prompt: Identify the type of coverage that checks if both the 'true' and 'false' paths of an 'if' statement have been tested.
